data.description: A recent study has found that approximately 25% of adults in the United States have experienced some form of cyber harassment or online abuse. The research surveyed over 2,000 individuals aged 18 and older. Of those surveyed, 60% reported receiving harassing messages, 45% reported having their personal information shared without consent, and 30% reported being threatened online. The study also revealed that women were more likely to experience cyber harassment compared to men. Additionally, it was discovered that individuals between the ages of 18-30 were the most targeted group for online abuse. The findings indicate a concerning trend of increasing cyber harassment affecting a significant portion of the adult population in the country.

data.title: Hideo Kojima to discuss Death Stranding 2 at Esports World Cup 2025 Conference in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.
data.description: A prominent game developer, recognized as a leading figure in the gaming sector, is scheduled to participate in the Esports World Cup 2025 Festival and deliver a speech at the New Global Sport Conference 2025 in RIYADH, Saudi Arabia on August 8, 2025. This event is organized by the Esports World Cup Foundation (EWCF), marking a significant moment in the gaming industry.

data.title: teamLab將於2025年10月7日在京都開設新館teamLab Biovortex Kyoto，占地面積超過1萬平方公尺，展出多件新藝術品。
data.description: In Kyoto, Japan, teamLab Biovortex Kyoto, a permanent art museum by the art group teamLab, is set to open in the southern district of Kyoto Prefecture on October 7, 2025, as part of a project in the southeastern area near Kyoto Station. This will be teamLab's largest museum in Japan, spanning over 10,000 square meters. To prepare for the opening, teamLab is creating several new art pieces, some of which have never been exhibited in Japan before. The first batch of announced works to be featured in the new museum includes Massless Amorphous Sculpture, Morphing Continuum, The Eternal Universe of Words, The Way of Birds, and Forest of Resonating Lamps. Massless Amorphous Sculpture, one of the pieces being exhibited for the first time in Japan, is based on teamLab's Environmental Phenomena concept, creating a floating giant sculpture through unique environmental phenomena. The piece is currently on display at teamLab Phenomena in Abu Dhabi and the Miami Art Center, with its first appearance in Japan scheduled for this exhibition. Tickets are now available on the official website.
